channel.title: Tech Insights
channel.description: Daily curated news on AI, developer tools, and the tech industry

item.title: "I Made AI Coding Tools Free (For Real This Time)"
item.description: The Problem You know what's funny about "free" AI coding tools? They're not free. Codex CLI says it uses your OpenAI credits — and those run out fast. Claude Code says it uses Anthropic — and...

item.title: React 19, Zustand, a 5-Provider AI Broker, and P2P Multiplayer — How I Architected My Learning Platform
item.description: Posted by Prateek | March 2026 I shipped React 19 in production when it was still RC. Not because I love living dangerously — well, maybe a little — but because I was building PatternMaster from...

item.title: I Turned Flashcards Into a Zombie FPS, a Runner Game, and Tetris — Here's How
item.description: Cross-posted from my dev.to series on building PatternMaster, an AI-powered DSA learning platform. Most "gamified" learning apps use games as a reward. Finish your lesson → get to play a little...

item.title: I Built an AI That Teaches You DSA Through Faded Parsons Problems and Spaced Repetition
item.description: By Prateek So I've been deep in this project for a while now and I keep getting asked "what exactly does it do?" by friends, and I keep giving this rambling five-minute answer that never quite...

item.title: We Re-Scanned the Top 50 ClawHub Skills — Things Have Changed
item.description: One week after our initial scan, we ran the numbers again. The ClawHub ecosystem has changed — fast. Total downloads across the Top 50 grew from 1.25M to over 3.5M in one week. The #1 skill now...

item.title: From Chaos to Clarity
item.description: How SpecKit Helped Me Structure the Way I Build Software The Moment I Realized Something Was Missing At some point in almost every growing codebase, the same pattern appears. A new feature is...

item.title: media workflows changed how i think about content pipelines
item.description: ok so i used to handle media (images, videos, pdfs) in the most manual way possible. upload to server, resize with imagemagick, convert format, move to CDN... every. single. time. then i...

item.title: Day 57 of #100DayOfCode — Understanding Server Actions vs API Routes & MongoDB in Next.js
item.description: API routing in Next.js enables developers to create backend endpoints directly within their applications, allowing for request handling, data processing, and API exposure. However, as apps become...

item.title: Claude Mythos Leak: Why Cybersec Pros + Tech Workers Should Be Nervous Right Now 😰
item.description: Claude Mythos leak puts AI security back in focus ⚠️ "I used to think until just a few days ago that AI wouldn't takeover cybersecurity — but now this leak has made me doubt my own words." A...

item.title: Will I lose my job because of GenAI?
item.description: Will I lose my job soon? Will my job be replaced by GenAI Agents? Many developers (and other professionals) might have that question in mind today. To be honest, I don't have the answer to these...

item.title: 150 million users later, Roblox competitor Rec Room is shutting down
item.description: Rec Room, a Roblox-like social gaming platform that lets users create games and experiences for others to play, is shutting down on June 1st. Despite reaching more than 150 million players and...

item.title: 15% of Americans say they’d be willing to work for an AI boss, according to new poll
item.description: According to a Quinnipiac University poll, 15% of Americans say they'd be willing to have a job where their direct supervisor was an AI program that assigned tasks and set schedules.

item.title: Here are our favorite spring cleaning deals from Amazon’s Big Spring Sale
item.description: Amazon’s Big Spring Sale is underway, so naturally, a lot of the deals are geared toward the season, including plenty of gadgets to help with spring cleaning. We’ve already rounded up a selection...

item.title: Popular AI gateway startup LiteLLM ditches controversial startup Delve
item.description: LiteLLM had obtained two security compliance certifications via Delve and fell victim to some horrific credential-stealing malware last week.

item.title: As more Americans adopt AI tools, fewer say they can trust the results
item.description: AI adoption is rising in the U.S., but trust remains low, with most Americans concerned about transparency, regulation, and the technology’s broader societal impact, according to a new Quinnipiac...

item.title: The first Apple Home Key-ready smart lock with UWB is cheaper during Amazon’s Big Spring Sale
item.description: There are so many great smart locks out there. But one of best models that iPhone owners should consider buying now is Aqara’s U400, which offers hands-free unlocking when you get close enough. It...

item.title: OpenAI Patches ChatGPT Data Exfiltration Flaw and Codex GitHub Token Vulnerability
item.description: A previously unknown vulnerability in OpenAI ChatGPT allowed sensitive conversation data to be exfiltrated without user knowledge or consent, according to new findings from Check Point. "A single...

item.title: Beats’ gym-friendly Powerbeats Fit are $30 off for Amazon’s Big Spring Sale
item.description: For iPhone owners, the AirPods Pro 3 are a fantastic all-around set of wireless earbuds, even for working out. Their value is especially tough to beat at $199.99; however, even with five sizes of...

item.title: A new RCS update could connect iPhone and Android video calls, someday
item.description: iPhone and Android users might be able to make interoperable video calls through their messaging apps - eventually. Last week, the GSM Association (GSMA) announced that the finalized RCS Universal...

item.title: Dyson’s ultra-thin PencilVac Fluffycones is tougher to resist at $150 off
item.description: Robot vacuums are great for keeping floors clean on a regular basis, but they don’t catch everything. If you’re looking for a tool to handle the finer details your robovac might miss — room...

item.title: A new manufacturing process uses lasers to seal paper packaging instead of glue
item.description: Although paper is a more environmentally friendly packaging material than plastic, it's often contaminated with additives, such as adhesives used to create a secure seal. That complicates the...

item.title: There are more AI health tools than ever—but how well do they work?
item.description: Earlier this month, Microsoft launched Copilot Health, a new space within its Copilot app where users will be able to connect their medical records and ask specific questions about their health. A...

item.title: The latest Pixel 11 leak shows slimmer bezels and an all-black camera bar
item.description: The launch of the Google Pixel 11 is still months away, but leaked renders shared by Android Headlines may give us an idea of what to expect. Based on the images, the Pixel 11 could come with...

item.title: DeepLoad Malware Uses ClickFix and WMI Persistence to Steal Browser Credentials
item.description: A new campaign has leveraged the ClickFix social engineering tactic as a way to distribute a previously undocumented malware loader referred to as DeepLoad. "It likely uses AI-assisted obfuscation...

item.title: The Pentagon’s culture war tactic against Anthropic has backfired
item.description: This story originally appeared in The Algorithm, our weekly newsletter on AI. To get stories like this in your inbox first, sign up here. Last Thursday, a California judge temporarily blocked the...

item.title: Okta’s CEO is betting big on AI agent identity
item.description: Today, I’m talking with Todd McKinnon, who is co-founder and CEO of Okta, a platform that lets big companies manage security and identity across all the apps and services their employees use....

item.title: The best deals we’ve found from Amazon’s Big Spring Sale (so far)
item.description: Amazon loves to manufacture an event. March is historically a dry spell for deals; however, with Amazon’s third annual Big Spring Sale, which runs through tomorrow, March 31st, the retail behemoth...

item.title: Mantis Biotech is making ‘digital twins’ of humans to help solve medicine’s data availability problem
item.description: Mantis takes disparate sources of data to make synthetic datasets that can be used to build so-called "digital twins" of the human body, representing anatomy, physiology and behavior.

item.title: ⚡ Weekly Recap: Telecom Sleeper Cells, LLM Jailbreaks, Apple Forces U.K. Age Checks and More
item.description: Some weeks are loud. This one was quieter but not in a good way. Long-running operations are finally hitting courtrooms, old attack methods are showing up in new places, and research that stopped...

item.title: ScaleOps raises $130M to improve computing efficiency amid AI demand
item.description: ScaleOps just raised $130M to tackle GPU shortages and soaring AI cloud costs by automating infrastructure in real time.
